name: Critical File Permission Changed
notes:
This rule triggers when permission of critical files or directories are modified. An attacker may modify the permissions of a sensitive file to become the only user able to access it and proceed to Defacement, Data Destruction, Disabling Security Tools, etc.

Note: Edit the "BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint" Building Block with files or directories to monitor. These locations should be related to boot, backup, logging, or credentials, which have a higher severity when exploited.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint
BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom)
conditions_text: when BB:CategoryDefinition: File Permission Changed, BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom), Log Source and different QID in 1 minutes
thresholds: when BB:CategoryDefinition: File Permission Changed, BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom), Log Source and different QID in 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Critical File Permission Changed on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:CategoryDefinition: File Permission Changed, BB:CategoryDefinition: Critical Files and Directories for Endpoint match at least 2 times with the same Record Number (custom), Log Source and different QID in 1 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Critical File Permission Changed
Event description: Detects when permission of critical files or directories are modified.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
